## Summary

`source_integrity.route` in `full_context_story_ledger_v1` was classifying Sister (4,903 words) as `LONG_FORM`. Two compounding bugs:

1. `inferSeedRoute(workType)` only checked the `workType` string label, ignoring word count entirely
2. `processor.ts` defaulted `work_type || 'novel'` for all manuscripts with null/empty work_type — so a 4,903-word manuscript got classified as a novel

**Fix:** `inferSeedRoute(workType, wordCount)` now returns `SHORT_FORM` for anything under 25K words regardless of work_type label. New `inferWorkTypeFromWordCount(wordCount)` classifies using standard publishing boundaries (flash <750, short_story <7,500, novelette <20K, novella <50K, novel ≥50K). All 11 instances of `work_type || 'novel'` in `processor.ts` replaced with `effectiveWorkType` computed once from the actual manuscript word count.

**Model upgrade:** Ledger model fallback changed from `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` → `EVAL_SYNTHESIS_MODEL`. Characters and relationships in the seed are ground truth for downstream — a weak seed cascades into weak evaluation.

## Scope

Pass selection (CHECK EXACTLY ONE — Pass 2 pre-checked as default):

- [x] Pass 1
- [ ] Pass 2
- [ ] Pass 3

Changed files:

- `lib/evaluation/seed/benchmarkContextBuilder.ts` — `inferSeedRoute` now accepts `wordCount`, added `inferWorkTypeFromWordCount`
- `lib/evaluation/seed/fullContextStoryLedger.ts` — passes `wordCount` to `buildFullContextSystemPrompt` → `inferSeedRoute`
- `lib/evaluation/seed/semanticSeedGenerator.ts` — added `wordCount` to input type, derives from manuscript text if not provided
- `lib/evaluation/processor.ts` — import `inferWorkTypeFromWordCount`, compute `effectiveWorkType` once, replace all 11 `|| 'novel'` instances
- `lib/evaluation/policy.ts` — `getCanonicalLedgerModel` fallback: `EVAL_SYNTHESIS_MODEL` instead of `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL`

Out of scope:

- Phase 1A/2/3 logic unchanged
- Chunk routing unchanged (already uses its own word count classification)

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

Process Change: no

## Contract Integrity

- `inferSeedRoute` signature extended with optional `wordCount` param — backward compatible
- `inferWorkTypeFromWordCount` is new, used only as fallback when `work_type` is null/empty
- Ledger model upgrade is configuration-level — no prompt or artifact schema changes

## Behavioral Quality

This PR is not reducing intelligence.

Model upgrade from cheap to synthesis tier for ledger generation improves character/relationship extraction depth. Route classification fix ensures short-form manuscripts get the correct evaluation template and benchmark context.

## Quality Gate / Anomalies

No QG_ behavior changes. Route classification is consumed by prompt context builder, not by quality gates.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Synthesis model costs more per ledger call than cheap model. Justified by seed quality being ground truth for all downstream phases.
